segments.text: India and the US just finalized a trade deal and it may give the Indian electronic sector a $100 billion opportunity. Here's what's actually
happening. The US lowered tariffs on Indian imports from 50% to 18% effective immediately [music] for India's electronics manufacturing sector. This
changes the competitive landscape. India now faces an 18% tariff while Vietnam faces 20%. That 2 percentage point
difference matters when global companies are deciding where to build supply chains. Think of it like this. When Apple decides where to manufacture
iPhones, tariff rates directly impact the final cost structure. Lower tariffs mean India becomes more competitive than alternatives. India already produces
nearly a quarter of all iPhones globally behind only China. The electronic sector employs over 2 million workers directly
and exported 38 billion worth of products in 2024 to 25 with the US being the largest market. Industry projections
suggest bilateral electronics trade could reach $100 billion as part of the broader $500 billion India US trade
target. What do you think about this deal? Let me know in the comments.
